claim: Tag explosion is a real anti-pattern: tagging every noun (#glue, #wood, #saw) creates noise, not signal; recommended remedies are lowercase + singular tags, autocomplete, and tagging sparingly.
quote: Tag sparingly — It's easy to overdo it with tags. Just like with backlinks, less is often more. Keep your tags all lowercase. Use singular tags. Utilize Autocomplete when tagging.

claim: A 2024 forum poster reports a recurring cycle of PKM bloat-then-restart: too many notes, too many ways to link them, leading to unusable vaults despite repeated fresh starts with simpler intentions.
quote: Every attempt, over decades, from pen and paper to Obsidian to Notion, has ended in a mess that becomes unusable. Once the system gets bloated, I start over with the best intentions, trying to keep things simple.

claim: Hacker News commenters describe Obsidian PKM over-engineering as cyclical influencer-driven complexity; remedy is to drop elaborate frameworks and use a minimal daily journal plus search.
quote: the more I learned, the more I realized a majority of it was cyclical... indulgence in it happens at the expense of getting any real work done.

claim: Tags suffer a granularity-ambiguity problem (dog vs dogs vs pets vs mammals) that breaks retrieval unless autocomplete or a controlled vocabulary is enforced.
quote: I think tags are confusing, for reasons that have been adequately discussed elsewhere (Do I tag this document 'dog'? 'dogs'? 'pets?' 'mammals?')

claim: Real users report frontmatter property sprawl from imports/plugins (200+ stale fields) and lack of native bulk-rename/remove tools, forcing error-prone manual cleanup.
quote: I had 200+ property fields...most of them are useless being able to remove properties would be great... I used a plugin to add date fields...the plugin didn't offer a way to remove the Properties it had created.

claim: Tag Wrangler's own docs warn that bulk tag renames are irreversible, can silently merge tags (losing provenance), and may partial-fail under sync; backup before renaming is the recommended remedy.
quote: Renaming a tag is potentially an irreversible operation: you may wish to back up your data before beginning a rename... If background sync causes files to change during a rename, Tag Wrangler may skip changed file(s), resulting in a partial rename.

claim: A 2024 user reflection recommends a controlled-vocabulary tag system (Library of Congress style) and reserves frontmatter properties strictly for objective minutiae (URLs, dates, author, series) to avoid sprawl.
quote: everything you're about to read is just me thinking out loud about how I've been using Obsidian lately after a lot of experimentation... Reserve [properties] for minutiae like URLs, publication date...author name, series, etc.

claim: Community guidance on the inline-vs-property-tag split: note-level classification belongs in frontmatter; sentence/section-level markup belongs inline as #tags, and tags should be migrated to stable fields once patterns stabilize.
quote: If you want to tag a note, it belongs in the frontmatter, if you want to tag a sentence or a section, it belongs in the text... transition tags into more stable fields once patterns emerge.

claim: The 2023 Obsidian Properties feature rollout broke existing tag-bearing frontmatter for many users — a concrete instance of metadata-rot from a platform schema change.
quote: Properties update broke my meta tags

claim: Users report phantom tag bloat where dozens of tags appear unprompted, illustrating that tag indexes can drift from actual content and require periodic auditing.
quote: Suddenly many dozens of tags I did not create

claim: A long-running multi-page feature request shows persistent demand for safe, native bulk tag rename/delete across files — confirming this is a chronic pain point, not a one-off.
quote: Tag Mass Action: Add, Rename, and Delete a tag in multiple files

claim: Forum threads document users hitting the wall of needing to rename a frontmatter property value vault-wide and resorting to ripgrep/sd or external scripts because no native command existed.
quote: How can I rename a frontmatter / property value in all my notes?
